lines.text: Tucker in the field, East Side, Schenk's Corners corridor. We're {{ELAPSED}} in and the analytics are climbing faster than forecast. Madison-wide count is forty-three confirmed, up nineteen percent from this point last year.

Multiple structure fires reported in the last thirty minutes. Smoke's carrying west across the isthmus — visibility dropping hard. Mendota shoreline's getting hazier. Caroline's weather call was good, but the wind shift's making the air worse, not better.

East Side's pulling thirty percent of the Madison total. Atwood Avenue, Schenk's Corners, the south Beltline juncture — three separate hotspots lighting up simultaneously. That's coordinated or coincidental, and I'm running the probability now and—

Bladed weapons up to twenty-six percent of kills this hour. Firearms down. Economic shift Holden called earlier is real-time confirmed. Cheaper, quieter, doesn't require ammunition people can't afford.
Visibility's under five miles now. I'm repositioning north toward the Capitol perimeter, got reports of secondary activity near the State Street approach and I need to get closer before the smoke—
Hold on, I'm moving through an intersection and there's a vehicle—

tickerItems.text: East Side surge: 13 kills this hour. Atwood + Schenk's Corners + South Beltline coordinated spikes.
Multiple structure fires reported. Smoke advisory: visibility degrading across isthmus. Wind shift westbound.
Bladed weapons now 26% of methodology. Firearms declining YoY trend continues.
